issues.title: `toolName cannot be null or empty` in  Flux
issues.body:
**Bug description**
I found an issue when making MCP calls, where an error occurs when the requesting controller returns Flux<> in reactive mode
```java
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: toolName cannot be null or empty
	at org.springframework.util.Assert.hasText(Assert.java:253) ~[spring-core-6.2.3.jar:6.2.3]
	Suppressed: reactor.core.publisher.FluxOnAssembly$OnAssemblyException: 
```

**Environment**
java: oracle openJDK17
Spring AI: 1.0.0-M6
LM Studio: 0.3.12
Model: Qwen2.5

**Steps to reproduce**
```shell
cd spring-mcp-test
git checkout EmptyToolName
mvn clean package
java -jar mcp-server/target/mcp-server.jar
java -jar mcp-client/target/mcp-client.jar
```
```shell
curl http://localhost:8080/ai/mcp?message=Get%scurrent%sDate
# or message=List the files in the UV directory of drive D
```

**Expected behavior**
Some information about files.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
Check out the project [https://github.com/fxz26284407/spring-mcp-test](https://github.com/fxz26284407/spring-mcp-test)

> I tried running the example file you posted and it returns a message like this
> 
> ```
> What specific help do you need regarding listing files? Please provide more details.%
> ```
> 
> No errors occurred
>

Sorry, the message parameter of the URL was written incorrectly. It should be like this
```shell
curl http://localhost:8080/ai/mcp?message=Get%scurrent%sDate
# or message=List the files in the UV directory of drive D
```

within the EmptyToolName branch I started the server and the client apps (using gpt-4o)

Then thit the http://localhost:8080/ai/mcp?message=Get%scurrent%sDate and got
`What specific directory would you like to list the files from?`

No errors

Assembly trace from producer [reactor.core.publisher.FluxDefer] :
	reactor.core.publisher.Flux.defer(Flux.java:810)
	org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$11(OpenAiChatModel.java:351)
Error has been observed at the following site(s):
	*__________Flux.defer ⇢ at org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$11(OpenAiChatModel.java:351)
	|_   Flux.subscribeOn ⇢ at org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$11(OpenAiChatModel.java:365)
	*________Flux.flatMap ⇢ at org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$14(OpenAiChatModel.java:349)
	|_     Flux.doOnError ⇢ at org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$14(OpenAiChatModel.java:371)
	|_     Flux.doFinally ⇢ at org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$14(OpenAiChatModel.java:372)

Original Stack Trace:
		at org.springframework.util.Assert.hasText(Assert.java:253) ~[spring-core-6.2.3.jar:6.2.3]
		at org.springframework.ai.tool.resolution.DelegatingToolCallbackResolver.resolve(DelegatingToolCallbackResolver.java:45) ~[spring-ai-core-1.0.0-20250313.104833-1351.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
		at org.springframework.ai.model.tool.DefaultToolCallingManager.lambda$executeToolCall$4(DefaultToolCallingManager.java:205) ~[spring-ai-core-1.0.0-20250313.104833-1351.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
		at java.base/java.util.Optional.orElseGet(Optional.java:364) ~[na:na]
		at org.springframework.ai.model.tool.DefaultToolCallingManager.executeToolCall(DefaultToolCallingManager.java:205) ~[spring-ai-core-1.0.0-20250313.104833-1351.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
		at org.springframework.ai.model.tool.DefaultToolCallingManager.executeToolCalls(DefaultToolCallingManager.java:138) ~[spring-ai-core-1.0.0-20250313.104833-1351.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
		at org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$10(OpenAiChatModel.java:354) ~[spring-ai-openai-1.0.0-20250313.104833-1259.jar:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT]
		at reactor.core.publisher.FluxDefer.subscribe(FluxDefer.java:46) ~[reactor-core-3.7.3.jar:3.7.3]
		at reactor.core.publisher.InternalFluxOperator.subscribe(InternalFluxOperator.java:68) ~[reactor-core-3.7.3.jar:3.7.3]
		at reactor.core.publisher.FluxSubscribeOn$SubscribeOnSubscriber.run(FluxSubscribeOn.java:194) ~[reactor-core-3.7.3.jar:3.7.3]
		at reactor.core.scheduler.WorkerTask.call(WorkerTask.java:84) ~[reactor-core-3.7.3.jar:3.7.3]
		at reactor.core.scheduler.WorkerTask.call(WorkerTask.java:37) ~[reactor-core-3.7.3.jar:3.7.3]
		at java.base/java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run$$$capture(FutureTask.java:264) ~[na:na]
		at java.base/java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java) ~[na:na]
		at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor$ScheduledFutureTask.run(ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor.java:304) ~[na:na]
		at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1136) ~[na:na]
		at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:635) ~[na:na]
		at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:833) ~[na:na]
```

I encountered the same issue and have identified the root cause. The problem occurs when processing chunks where the tool call ID is an empty string rather than null. In the OpenAiStreamFunctionCallingHelper class, there's a critical section of code: ```java if (current.toolCalls() != null) { if (current.toolCalls().size() > 1) { throw new IllegalStateException("Currently only one tool call is supported per message!"); } var currentToolCall = current.toolCalls().iterator().next(); if (currentToolCall.id() != null) { // <--- HERE is the problem! This check passes for empty strings if (lastPreviousTooCall != null) { toolCalls.add(lastPreviousTooCall); } toolCalls.add(currentToolCall); } else { toolCalls.add(merge(lastPreviousTooCall, currentToolCall)); // <--- This merging code is skipped for empty strings } } ``` When a chunk contains a tool call with an empty string ID, the condition `currentToolCall.id() != null` evaluates to true (since empty string ≠ null), causing it to execute the wrong branch. This prevents proper merging of the tool calls that should be combined. This eventually leads to the `java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: toolName cannot be null or empty` exception because the function in the chunk doesn't contain the name field information, which would have been available if the tool calls were properly merged.

issues.title: `Multiple tools with the same name` in reactive mode
issues.body:
**Bug description**
I found an issue when making MCP calls, where an error occurs when the requesting controller returns Flux<> in reactive mode
```java
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Multiple tools with the same name (getDayOfWeek, getCurrentDate) found in ToolCallingChatOptions
	at org.springframework.ai.model.tool.ToolCallingChatOptions.validateToolCallbacks(ToolCallingChatOptions.java:227) ~[spring-ai-core-1.0.0-M6.jar:1.0.0-M6]
	Suppressed: reactor.core.publisher.FluxOnAssembly$OnAssemblyException: 
```

**Environment**
java: oracle openJDK17
Spring AI: 1.0.0-M6
LM Studio: 0.3.12
Model: Qwen2.5


**Steps to reproduce**
```shell
cd spring-mcp-test
mvn clean package
java -jar mcp-server/target/mcp-server.jar
java -jar mcp-client/target/mcp-client.jar
```
```shell
curl http://localhost:8080/ai/mcp?message=listfiles
```

**Expected behavior**
Some information about files.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
Check out the project [https://github.com/fxz26284407/spring-mcp-test](https://github.com/fxz26284407/spring-mcp-test)

comments.body:
I have the same problem. i try to debug for reason because open the mcp client  have two instance. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8e513d72-0559-4830-9ca7-fef47e43c0e9) 

and also use the Spring AI: 1.0.0-M6

Resolved by 3f7f2f1bffade3d6029dc6bca9631033533273d8 and 957150128b13c03e0381941b18eb56df8979ddf6 
Feel free to re-open if the issue is still present.

## Issue Description

When using Spring AI 1.0.0-M6, I noticed a difference in behavior between SYNC and ASYNC modes regarding tool registration:

- With `type: ASYNC`: The application starts successfully even with duplicate tools
- With `type: SYNC`: The application throws `Multiple tools with the same name` exception

This seems related to issue #2422, which was marked as resolved. However, the behavior difference between SYNC and ASYNC modes might need further investigation.

### Environment
- Spring AI version: 1.0.0-M6
- Java version: JDK 17
- OS: Windows 10

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Configure a Spring AI application with duplicate tool registrations
2. Try running with `type: SYNC` - Results in error
3. Try running with `type: ASYNC` - Works without error

### Error Message

ver: 1.0.0-M6
my situation is:
there are two io.modelcontextprotocol.client.McpAsyncClient in List<McpAsyncClient> mcpClients,
they have same clientInfo,
one 's transport is io.modelcontextprotocol.client.transport.**WebFluxSseClientTransport**
the other one is io.modelcontextprotocol.client.transport.**HttpClientSseClientTransport**
and my mcp client and server are both using webflux:
```
client:
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-ai-mcp-client-webflux-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
</dependency>
```
```
server:
<dependency>
	<groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
	<artifactId>spring-ai-mcp-server-webflux-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

> ver: 1.0.0-M6 我的情况是： List mcpClients 中有两个 io.modelcontextprotocol.client.McpAsyncClient， 它们具有相同的 clientInfo， 一个的传输是 io.modelcontextprotocol.client.transport. **WebFluxSseClientTransport** 另一个是 io.modelcontextprotocol.client.transport. **HttpClientSseClientTransport** 并且我的 mcp 客户端和服务器都使用 webflux：
> 
> ```
> client:
> <dependency>
>     <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
>     <artifactId>spring-ai-mcp-client-webflux-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
> </dependency>
> ```
> 
> ```
> server:
> <dependency>
> 	<groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
> 	<artifactId>spring-ai-mcp-server-webflux-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
> </dependency>
> ```

The problem has been resolved, please check #2493

comments.body:
i met this error too, and the reason is when the function has no params
**stream call toolCall.arguments field returned "".** but **non-stream call returned "{}"** 

 `org.springframework.ai.tool.method.MethodToolCallback`.call() and `org.springframework.ai.tool.function.FunctionToolCallback`.call() has code:
```
public String call(String toolInput, @Nullable ToolContext toolContext) {
		Assert.hasText(toolInput, "toolInput cannot be null or empty");
```

I don't know if this is a problem of LLM or client should compatible this.
